briefs.title: Council to Vote on Accepting Donated Lease Rights for Craneway Pavilion Property
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a donation agreement under which Orton Entertainment, LLC would hand over its leasehold interests — meaning its rights to occupy and use the property under existing leases — at the historic Craneway Pavilion waterfront site, along with some personal property and $80,000 in cash. However, a third party (PB Development Group) has filed a lawsuit related to the property and is urging Council to delay approval until legal disputes are resolved.
briefs.action_required: If your business has ties to the Craneway Pavilion property, attend the April 21 Council meeting to have your say.

key_dates.label: Craneway Pavilion donation agreement escrow close date

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Vote on Accepting Donated Lease for Historic Craneway Property in Richmond
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on accepting a donation from Orton Entertainment, LLC — the company would hand over its lease rights to the Craneway Pavilion at 1414–1422 Harbor Way South, along with $80,000 in cash. If approved, the City would take on full responsibility for the building, including an estimated $600K–$1.67M in annual operating costs and up to $12.7M in long-term repair needs.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 21 council vote — this decision could affect event venue, hospitality, and waterfront business opportunities in Richmond.

key_dates.label: Craneway Donation Agreement escrow closing deadline — Richmond Council

briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider New Taxes and Fees to Fund Advanced Life Support and Fire Station Upgrades
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to review a report on how to pay for Advanced Life Support (ALS) first responder services and aging fire station upgrades. Council will consider options including a parcel tax (an annual charge on each property), voter-approved bonds, and service fees — and may decide whether to put a funding measure on the November 2026 ballot.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — a new parcel tax or fee could affect your property costs.

key_dates.label: Richmond ALS funding measure — potential November 2026 ballot vote

briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Healthcare, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on $900,000 contract for wastewater and stormwater compliance support
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a sole-source contract — meaning only one supplier is being considered, without a competitive bid — with Larry Walker & Associates for up to $900,000 over three years (April 2026–April 2029). The contract would keep the city meeting state and federal rules for wastewater treatment, stormwater management, and sewer system operations.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this affects how the city manages water compliance costs.

key_dates.label: Larry Walker & Associates wastewater compliance contract start date
Larry Walker & Associates wastewater compliance contract end date

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Receive March 2026 Abatement Report on Illegal Dumping and Encampments
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to receive a monthly update from the Public Works Department covering cleanup activities in March 2026. The report covers illegal dumping removal, encampment cleanups, graffiti removal, weed abatement, and vehicle dismantling — including 198.89 tons of illegally dumped material and 19 encampment sites cleared across the city.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on buying concrete barriers and boulders for encampment mitigation in Richmond
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving purchases of K-Rails (heavy concrete barriers) and natural stone boulders from two suppliers — First Vanguard Rentals & Sales, Inc. and Blue Angel International LLC. Each contract would run three years at up to $450,000 per vendor, with options to extend for two more years at up to $150,000 per year, to block unauthorized access and manage encampments on public land.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 21, 2026 — this could affect public spaces near your business.

key_dates.label: Richmond Council vote — K-Rail and boulder purchase approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on accepting 7th Street Bike Lane project as complete
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on formally accepting the 7th Street Bike Lane Improvement Project as finished. The vote would also approve an increase to the construction contract with Rapid Grading — raising the total payment limit to $420,038.73 — and authorize the release of bonds and held-back payments (money withheld until work is confirmed complete) to the contractor.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 7th Street between Lincoln Avenue and Hensley Street, note that construction is wrapping up.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Vote on Accepting Craneway Pavilion Lease Transfer from Orton Entertainment
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a donation agreement under which Orton Entertainment, LLC would hand over its leasehold interests (the right to use and occupy the property under a long-term lease) at the historic Craneway Pavilion waterfront site to the City, along with personal property and $80,000 in cash. The City would gain direct control of the site, avoiding a costly lawsuit over unpaid maintenance obligations — though a third-party business, PB Development Group, has raised legal concerns about ongoing disputes tied to the property.
briefs.action_required: If your business has ties to the Craneway Pavilion site, attend the council meeting and speak during public comment.

key_dates.label: Craneway Pavilion lease transfer escrow closing deadline

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider Annual Update on Sanctuary City Contracting and Investment Policy Compliance
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to receive a written update from the Finance Department on how the city is complying with its Sanctuary City Contracting and Investment Policy Ordinance. This policy bans the city from hiring or investing in companies that provide data brokering or 'extreme vetting' services — meaning tools that help identify or track people — to U.S. immigration enforcement (ICE).
briefs.action_required: If you contract with the City of Richmond, confirm you do not provide data or vetting services to ICE.
briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Technology, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Accepting Craneway Pavilion lease donation from Orton Entertainment
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a Donation Agreement under which Orton Entertainment, LLC would hand over its leasehold interests (the right to use and occupy the property under a long-term lease) at the historic Craneway Pavilion, plus $80,000 cash, to the City. A public commenter has raised concerns that an active $10-million lawsuit and unresolved contractual disputes may complicate the transfer.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates at or near Craneway Pavilion, monitor the council vote — ownership control of the site is changing.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Accepting $9,000 State Library Grant for Summer Lunch Program
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on accepting a $9,000 grant from the California State Library to fund the 'Lunch at the Library' program. If approved, the money would be used to buy books in English, Spanish, and other languages for kids at summer meal sites across Richmond, starting in June 2026.

key_dates.label: Lunch at the Library book distribution anticipated to begin

briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on $600,000 contract for free youth summer camp program
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a three-year contract worth up to $600,000 with the West Contra Costa Public Education Fund to run a free 8-week summer camp for about 300 local youth. The program, funded by a community grant, would serve elementary and middle school students at four community centres, focusing on reading and language skills.
briefs.industries: Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Accepting Craneway Pavilion lease transfer from Orton Entertainment
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a Donation Agreement that would transfer leasehold interests (the right to use and control the property under a long-term lease) in the Craneway Pavilion from Orton Entertainment, LLC to the City, along with personal property and an $80,000 cash contribution. A third party, PB Development Group, has filed a $10 million lawsuit related to operations at the site and is urging Council to delay approval until legal disputes are resolved.
briefs.action_required: If your business has ties to Craneway Pavilion, attend the April 21 Council meeting and submit public comment.

key_dates.label: Craneway Pavilion lease transfer escrow close date

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider Proposed Moratorium on New Data Centers in Richmond
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will consider directing city staff to develop a proposed temporary moratorium — a short-term pause — on new data center development. The pause would give the city time to study the impacts of data centers on energy use, water supply, noise, and neighbourhood compatibility before setting permanent rules.
briefs.action_required: If you operate or plan to develop a data center in Richmond, monitor this proposal closely — it could pause new approvals.

key_dates.label: Richmond data center moratorium proposal — Council meeting date

briefs.industries: Technology, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to Vote on: Accepting Donated Lease Rights for Craneway Pavilion Waterfront Site
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a donation agreement under which Orton Entertainment, LLC would hand over its leasehold interests — meaning its legal right to occupy and use the property under existing leases — at the historic Craneway Pavilion waterfront site, along with some personal property and $80,000 in cash. The City would gain full control of the site, avoiding a costly lawsuit over unpaid maintenance obligations, though a third-party business (PB Development Group) has raised concerns about an active $10 million lawsuit and unresolved disputes that could complicate the transfer.
briefs.action_required: If your business has ties to the Craneway Pavilion site, attend the Council meeting to put your concerns on the record.

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to vote on supporting California bill protecting school funding during wildfire closures
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution formally supporting California Senate Bill 959 (SB 959). The proposed bill would let local fire agencies officially declare a major safety hazard, allowing schools to close during extreme wildfire emergencies without losing their state funding — which is currently tied to how many students show up each day.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the April 21, 2026 Richmond City Council meeting for the vote outcome on SB 959 support.

key_dates.label: Richmond City Council vote — SB 959 wildfire school safety resolution

briefs.industries: Professional Services, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider Fee Waiver for 2026 Mother's Day Event at Richmond Auditorium
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on co-sponsoring a Mother's Day community event on May 9, 2026, organized by the United Business Organization (UBO). The proposal would waive the auditorium rental fee — estimated at $8,280.50 — so UBO can host a free celebration for 300+ attendees at the Richmond Memorial Auditorium.
briefs.action_required: If your business wants to support or participate in this community event, contact UBO or a council sponsor before May 9.

key_dates.label: Richmond Mother's Day Community Event at Memorial Auditorium

briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Vote on Letter Urging Governor to Crack Down on Illegal Fireworks
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on sending a letter to California Governor Gavin Newsom, asking him to create a task force that would explore a formal agreement between California and Nevada to stop illegal fireworks from crossing state lines. The proposed agreement would use a shared database of fireworks sales records to track purchases made by California residents at Nevada stores.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it could lead to stricter tracking of fireworks purchases near state lines.

key_dates.label: Richmond City Council vote on illegal fireworks letter — public meeting

briefs.industries: Retail, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on updating Richmond's city-wide records retention and destruction schedules
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving updated rules for how long the city keeps — and when it destroys — official records across all departments (except the Police Department, which will be handled separately later). The update aims to cut storage and labour costs, reduce duplicate filing, and bring the city's record-keeping in line with current laws and technology.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it affects how long the city holds records that may relate to your business.

key_dates.label: Richmond City Council vote — records retention schedule update

briefs.industries: Professional Services, Technology, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Vote: Proposed Donation of Craneway Pavilion Lease from Private Developer to City
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on accepting a proposed 'donation' of leasehold interests (the right to use and control property under a long-term lease) at the historic Craneway Pavilion waterfront site from Orton Entertainment, LLC, along with personal property and $80,000 cash. The proposal would give the City full control of the site, but a third-party business (PB Development Group) has filed a $10M+ lawsuit over the same property, raising unresolved legal risks.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates at or near Craneway Pavilion, monitor the council vote and consult a lawyer about how the ownership transfer may affect your rights.

briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond to ban city resources from being used in immigration enforcement
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed ordinance (a local law) that would update city rules to clearly ban the use of city property, staff, and resources for civil immigration enforcement — meaning enforcement actions by federal immigration authorities like ICE. If approved, city facilities could not be used as staging areas or detention sites, and staff would receive training on the new rules.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — if you employ immigrant workers, this policy may affect how federal enforcement operates in your area.

key_dates.label: Richmond ICE-Free Zone Ordinance second reading — council vote
Richmond ICE-Free Zone Ordinance takes effect (if approved)

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Professional Services, Retail

briefs.title: Council to vote on adding caste as a protected category in Richmond anti-discrimination rules
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution and a proposed bylaw (a local law) that would add 'caste' as a protected category under the city's Human Rights and Human Relations Commission. Caste refers to a social ranking system based on family background — the proposal would mean residents could formally report caste-based discrimination in areas like employment, housing, education, and public services.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your workplace anti-discrimination policies.

key_dates.label: Richmond caste equity resolution and ordinance — Council vote (first reading)

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Professional Services, Landlord & Property Mgmt

The deal is proposed as an alternative to costly litigation over unpaid maintenance obligations, and would give the City full control of the historic waterfront property.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Routine Consent Calendar Items — Richmond, April 2026
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a group of routine items bundled together under a 'Consent Calendar' — a process where multiple non-controversial matters are approved in a single motion without individual debate. No specific bylaw or business-affecting measure has been detailed in the available agenda text.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Richmond Council agenda for April 21, 2026 to see if any item affects your business.
briefs.industries: Professional Services, Retail, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Richmond Open Forum for Public Comment — April 21, 2026
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to hold an open public comment session on April 21, 2026, where residents and business owners can speak to items on the Consent Calendar (a group of routine agenda items voted on together) or raise topics not listed on the agenda. Each speaker is limited to 2 minutes, and back-and-forth conversation with Council members is not permitted.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 21 Richmond Council meeting to share your comments on agenda items or local concerns.

key_dates.label: Richmond Council open public comment forum — attendance opportunity

briefs.industries: Professional Services, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider Receiving APWA Best Project Award for Richmond Bikeway Project
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to formally receive the 2026 APWA Northern California Chapter Best Project Award for the Ferry to Bridge to Greenway (F2B2G) project. The award recognizes the city's delivery of 2.25 miles of protected bike lanes connecting the Richmond Ferry Terminal, the Richmond Greenway, and the Richmond–San Rafael Bridge Trail — completed on time and on budget.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics

The City views this as a way to avoid costly litigation over deferred maintenance and to gain direct control of the property for public benefit.
